title: Comprehensive website audit of thestrategicveteran.com vs Adam Peters online presence
job:
You are doing a comprehensive audit of thestrategicveteran.com against everything publicly available about Adam Peters and The Strategic Veteran online.

## Step 1: Web Research on Adam Peters

Use brave-search to research the following — save your findings as you go:

- "Adam Peters Strategic Veteran" — articles, interviews, press coverage
- "The Strategic Veteran podcast" — episode listings, guest mentions, reviews, podcast directories
- "Adam Peters veteran transition" — any articles, features, mentions
- "Adam Peters Heroes Media Group"
- "Adam Peters LinkedIn" — any public posts or mentions indexed
- "Adam Peters speaker" or "Adam Peters keynote"
- "thestrategicveteran.com" — any external links, references, citations
- "Adam Peters AI consulting" or "Adam Peters CAIO"
- "Adam Peters Amakaya" or "Adam Peters plant medicine"
- "Unfuck Your Transition" — tagline mentions, coverage
- Any other relevant search variations you think of

Compile everything found: articles, podcast directories, episode counts, guest names, press features, credentials mentioned, social proof, external descriptions of what TSV is.

## Step 2: Crawl thestrategicveteran.com

Use the browser to visit and document every page of thestrategicveteran.com:
- Homepage
- About page
- Podcast/episodes page
- Any blog or resources section
- Any store or products page
- Any speaking or consulting page
- Contact page
- Any other pages you find

For each page, document:
- What content currently exists
- What it says about Adam, TSV, the podcast
- Any outdated information (old episode counts, old credentials, stale dates)
- What's missing that should be there
- Broken links or missing sections

## Step 3: Cross-Reference & Gap Analysis

Compare what the internet says about Adam Peters vs. what the website currently presents. Identify:

- Episodes/content that exist but aren't on the site
- Credentials, certifications, or affiliations mentioned online but not on the site
- Press coverage or social proof not featured
- New ventures (AI consulting, speaking, Amakaya) with no web presence
- Pages that need to be built from scratch
- Content that's outdated vs. current reality

## Step 4: Write the Audit Report

Save a comprehensive report to `data/website-audit.md` with the following structure:

```
# thestrategicveteran.com — Full Audit Report
Date: [today]

## What We Found Online (Adam Peters Web Presence)
[Summary of all research findings — articles, coverage, podcast reach, external descriptions]

## Current Website State (Page by Page)
[What each page currently contains]

## Gap Analysis: Reality vs. Website
[Specific mismatches between what exists in the world and what the site shows]

## Pages That Need to Be Built
[New pages needed: speaking, AI consulting, store, blog, episode archive, etc.]

## Prioritized Fix List
### Quick Wins (low effort, high impact)
### Medium Lifts
### Major Rebuilds

## Raw Research Notes
[Everything found during web research, for reference]
```

Be thorough. This report will be used to drive a full website rebuild. Don't summarize — capture everything. Flag anything surprising or that represents a missed opportunity.

Commit the completed report to the repo.
